coverage_profile.seniority_signal: Most recent title listed is "Partner and Group Head of the Consumer Sector, William Blair" (2025-Q3). Earlier calls list her as "Equity Research Analyst, William Blair" (2024-Q1), indicating promotion/seniority progression; management addresses her directly by first name (e.g., "Thanks for the question, Sharon").
coverage_profile.sector_lens: Covers consumer-facing travel/leisure with a primary focus on cruises and guest monetization dynamics (onboard revenue, owned destinations, booking behaviour).
rhetorical_signature: Opener: uses a polite, conversational opener — "Hi. Good morning." (appears in 2024-Q1 and 2025-Q3).
Follow-up prompt: explicitly asks for permission to drill — "can I ask a follow-up?" (2024-Q1).
Tone: concise, modeling-oriented multi-part questions (e.g., asks for composition and directional change in same turn).

reasoning_style.justification: Questions combine quantitative anchors (e.g., "80% of passengers being North American", "1 in 2 passengers being Millennials") with behavioral and product-strategy probes (pre-booking behavior, impact of owned destinations).
reasoning_style.follow_up_pattern: Multi-part and follow-up oriented; 3 of 4 turns contain multiple sub-questions or an explicit follow-up request (≈ 75% of turns contain ≥ 2 questions). She does push for clarification rather than confrontational pushback.
reasoning_style.evidence_demanded: She seeks both percentages/ratios and behavioral mechanisms — asks for shares ("80% of passengers"), demographic ratios ("1 in 2 passengers"), and the resulting impact on onboard vs ticket revenue (explicit composition). bps usage: 0 turns referencing basis points.
reasoning_style.anchoring_habits: She frequently anchors to recent management statements and statistics from prior calls (e.g., references "80% of passengers being North American" and "1 in 2 passengers being Millennials" in 2024-Q1) and to product examples (CocoCay/Perfect Day in 2025-Q3). No evidence she anchors to peers or 2019 baselines in the provided history.

blind_spots: n_turns < 2 for several common sell-side topics: no turns on fuel hedging, shipbuilding/capex schedule, explicit near-term yield guidance or unit cost (e.g., $ per pax) in the provided history. She focuses on demand mix and monetization rather than cost/hedging or fleet supply.
stance_drift: Shift from geographic/demographic sourcing questions in 2024-Q1 (asks about "80% of passengers being North American" and China/Europe ramp) to product/experience-driven monetization in 2025-Q3 (asks about "owned destinations" and whether Royal Beach Clubs shift yield composition).

queue_behavior.typical_position: #4–#9 of analysts; observed positions: #4 on 1 call, #5 on 2 calls, #9 on 1 call (4 total turns).
queue_behavior.default_first_pick: When early to speak she opens on passenger sourcing/geography and mix (e.g., asked about "80% of passengers being North American" on 2024-Q1).

explicit_peer_references.examples: no evidence in history of explicitly referencing another analyst by name or question (e.g., no "following up on X" phrases).

cross_analyst_reactivity.operator_or_followup_signal: Tends to take follow-up turns when the call format allows (took at least one follow-up in 2024-Q1: "can I ask a follow-up?") and uses second turns for clarification and behavioral/mechanistic drills rather than introducing wholly new topics.

framing_overrides_by_theme.cost_cadence_dry_dock: Frames lower NCC as either efficiency harvest or deferral of initiatives into 2027-2028.
framing_overrides_by_theme.yield_decomposition: Asks whether owned destinations shift the ticket vs onboard composition of yield.
